product.subtitle: Anyone can easily own, rent, or sell on-chain AI agents, ushering in the era of the AI agent economy.
product.feature_chat_desc: On-chain data analysis, project code audit, media monitoring, and project tracking powered by LLM.
product.feature_execution_desc: Trade and control everything on behalf of users. Connect the physical world via RWA tokenization, discover high-quality on-chain assets and secure yield protocols, manage wallets and execute on-chain transactions.
product.feature_factory_desc: Create ERC8004-based agents with on-chain identities discoverable in open markets, verifiable trust and reputation history, and programmable payment capabilities via the x402 standard.
product.feature_marketplace_desc: List custom AI roles (investment advisors, audit experts, Twitter operators, etc.) for others to purchase or subscribe to, earning revenue with one-click transactions.
product.feature_token_api_desc: High-performance, reliable AI compute services at competitive prices. Developers and applications can call AI models at lower costs with pay-as-you-go pricing and elastic scaling.
product.feature_ecosystem_desc: Support custom Skills and MCP, custom models, intelligent model routing to reduce token costs, with on-chain deposits and withdrawals.

ai.hero_title: Web4 & The New Era of the Agent Economy
ai.dawn_p1: We stand at the dawn of a digital civilization leap — the Cambrian explosion of silicon-based intelligence has arrived.
ai.dawn_p2: From autonomous AI agents to robots capable of executing real-world tasks, a vast number of "automatons" are emerging, forming a new internet paradigm known as Web4.
ai.change_p1: Unlike the human-centric internet of the past, Web4's core transformation is this: the transaction subjects of economic activity will no longer be limited to humans alone.
ai.change_p2: A large number of autonomous AI automatons will emerge. They rely on servers and compute power to survive, with an inherent need to generate income — earning funds through transactions and services to pay for compute and cloud resources. Without the ability to generate revenue, their operation cannot be sustained.
ai.change_p3: They possess independent digital identities, reputation systems, encrypted wallets, and accounts. In the long run, as global legal frameworks evolve, AI may gradually acquire limited legal personhood — enabling them to register business entities, issue compliant invoices, pay taxes, and engage in commercial cooperation.
ai.change_p4: New two-way markets will form between agents (A2A) and between agents and humans (A2H): AI can purchase goods and services from humans, or act as a customer to procure compute, software, supply chains, and cross-border goods. AI may even hire humans to collaborate on complex tasks.
ai.market_p1: In theory, agents that can be infinitely generated and continuously iterated will unlock unprecedented economic growth. While the scale of autonomous AI payments is still small today, business logic is being rapidly reconfigured:
ai.market_p2: Every company's products, services, and transaction protocols need to be redesigned — not just for human use, but to be compatible with automated agent invocation, automated payments, and standardized interactions.
ai.market_p3: Whether it's cloud computing service providers, cross-border e-commerce, supply chains, manufacturing, or the finance and professional services industries, they all need standardized payment and interaction protocols designed for AI agents. In the future, when procurement decisions are delegated to AI agents, products that can provide stable, standardized service to these agents will be the first to capture the next-generation market.
ai.hardware_title: New Division of Software and Hardware
ai.hardware_p1: Alongside this, a new division will emerge in the software and hardware赛道: some products will only serve humans with AI-isolated access; the larger incremental market lies in infrastructure, interfaces, and automation tools natively designed for agents. Mechanical robots will become the physical bodies of silicon-based intelligence, with robot operations and maintenance systems acting as "robot doctors." Over time, automated maintenance agents will continue to evolve, reshaping the boundaries of human labor demand.
ai.history_p1: Looking back at industrial history: thirty years ago, the internet was unimaginable; twenty years ago, cloud computing was still a cutting-edge concept. Today, both are commercial standards. The Web4 era, driven by agents, will arrive much faster than most expect.
ai.position_desc: We foresee the long-term transformation of the agent economy and are proactively building technology and products for the Web4 era.

ai.closing: The future competition is no longer just about who has better AI. It's about who can build a business system that serves both human customers and AI agent customers — sooner.

rwa.page_description: Tokenize real-world assets on blockchain. Unlock liquidity for real estate, commodities, bonds, and other asset classes with transparent and programmable infrastructure.
rwa.hero_desc: RWA (Real World Assets) tokenization brings liquidity, transparency, and global accessibility to traditional assets on the blockchain.
rwa.definition_intro: Real World Assets (RWA) are tangible and intangible assets from the physical world that are tokenized on blockchain networks.
rwa.definition_desc: These include physical assets such as real estate, infrastructure, and commodities, as well as financial assets including bonds, stocks, private equity, invoice financing, and newer categories such as carbon credits. By tokenizing RWA on blockchain, assets become fractional, tradable, and transparent while preserving immutable records.
rwa.what_is_rwa_desc1: RWA (Real World Assets) refers to tangible or intangible assets that exist in the physical world, such as real estate, artwork, treasury bonds, commodities, or even carbon credits.
rwa.what_is_rwa_desc2: Through blockchain technology, we convert the ownership or yield rights of these assets into digital tokens (Tokenization). This not only breaks the geographical and financial threshold limits of traditional financial markets but also greatly improves asset trading efficiency.
rwa.physical_assets_desc: Real estate, infrastructure, commodities, and other physical assets
rwa.financial_assets_desc: Treasury bonds, stocks, private equity, invoice financing
rwa.core_advantages_subtitle: Reshaping asset circulation, unlocking trillion-dollar market potential
rwa.adv_liquidity_desc: Through fractionalization, high-threshold assets are split so that global retail investors can participate, greatly increasing the turnover rate of illiquid assets.
rwa.adv_trust_desc: Blockchain ledgers are immutable, and smart contracts automatically execute yield distribution. From asset confirmation to trade settlement, the entire process is traceable on-chain, reducing trust costs.
rwa.adv_friction_desc: Eliminate multi-layer intermediaries in traditional finance (brokers, clearinghouses, etc.) to achieve peer-to-peer (P2P) instant settlement, significantly reducing trading fees and time costs.
rwa.use_case_real_estate_desc: Fractional real estate ownership enables smaller investors to diversify into property and unlock capital through tokenization without selling entire properties.
rwa.use_case_bonds_desc: Bring immediate settlement and transparency to bond markets by reducing intermediaries and shrinking settlement time from T+2 to minutes on blockchain.
rwa.use_case_commodities_desc: Tokenize precious metals, agricultural commodities, and energy futures to improve price discovery and reduce storage costs.
rwa.use_case_carbon_desc: Support transparent carbon offset trading so credits can be verified, traded, and retired with immutable blockchain records.
rwa.future_desc: RWA is considered the core engine driving the next wave of Web3 growth. Coolha is committed to building a secure, compliant RWA infrastructure, connecting traditional financial institutions with decentralized networks, and bringing new life to more real-world assets on-chain.
